title: malus communis var. domestica
intro: Malus communis var. domestica, commonly known as the domesticated apple, is a deciduous tree cultivated for its edible fruit and has been selectively bred over thousands of years for desirable traits. The medicinal properties of the apple plant are attributed to the presence of various compounds such as quercetin, catechins, and chlorogenic acid, which have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of consuming apples include supporting digestive health, reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and diabetes, and promoting overall well-being. Apple leaves, bark, and fruit can be used to prepare herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support respiratory health, alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, and provide a natural remedy for sore throats.
